notes: Phase 0 regression fixture for the Ireland livability model.
Ranges are intentionally broad in v1 and should only move through deliberate, reviewable git changes.
CI validates fixture structure only; full score assertions require a completed local precompute build.

locations.name: Terenure, Dublin
locations.rationale: Quiet, established suburb with everyday services, parks, and healthcare nearby; this should outrank many noisier city-core cells.

locations.name: Blackrock, Dublin
locations.rationale: Coastal suburb with real service density and pleasantness; a strong livability control rather than an extreme outlier.

locations.name: Malahide, Dublin
locations.rationale: Serviced coastal suburb with shops, rail access, and amenities in a calmer setting than the urban core.

locations.name: Sutton, Dublin
locations.rationale: Pleasant coastal suburb that should score respectably thanks to real services and environment, even if not every amenity is at the front door.

locations.name: Ballyhackamore, Belfast
locations.rationale: A serviced Belfast suburb with a believable mix of daily needs and a calmer feel than the city centre.

locations.name: Temple Bar, Dublin
locations.rationale: Extremely convenient but noisy and intense; it should not land near the very top of a livability ranking.

locations.name: Drumcondra rail corridor, Dublin
locations.rationale: Walkable and useful, but rail-line noise and traffic should keep it below quieter suburbs with comparable services.

locations.name: Sydenham near Belfast City Airport
locations.rationale: Nominally well connected, but airport and corridor noise should drag it down versus calmer areas.

locations.name: Dock Road corridor, Limerick
locations.rationale: Useful urban corridor with traffic and industrial-adjacent downsides; should be middling rather than elite.

locations.name: Barack Obama Plaza, M7
locations.rationale: A classic single-site amenity cluster trap: several services co-located, but not a real walkable neighbourhood.

locations.name: Lusk Services, M1
locations.rationale: Another motorway-cluster trap that should not score like a genuine suburb or town centre.

locations.name: Birdhill service cluster
locations.rationale: Multiple services at one site should count as limited convenience, not as a fully livable district.

locations.name: Kinvara, Galway
locations.rationale: Pleasant small-place living with some real services; should score respectably despite not being amenity-dense.

locations.name: Schull, Cork
locations.rationale: Low-service but attractive coastal settlement that should not collapse to the bottom of the scale.

locations.name: Roundstone, Galway
locations.rationale: Quiet and scenic with limited services; it should beat harsh motorway clusters while staying below real-service towns.

locations.name: Castlewellan, County Down
locations.rationale: A calm small-town setting with credible day-to-day amenities; respectable but not top-tier.

locations.name: Mullaghmeen rural stop area, Longford
locations.rationale: Sparse rural context where nominal stop presence should not inflate the score above real service towns.

locations.name: Glencolmcille, Donegal
locations.rationale: Remote and beautiful, but weak everyday access means it should not leapfrog properly serviced settlements.

locations.name: Riverstick rural stop area, Cork
locations.rationale: Nominal transport presence in a rural setting should still score below towns with reliable services.

locations.name: Jonesborough border rural area
locations.rationale: Some connections exist, but sparse service reality should keep this well below clearly serviced town centres.

locations.name: Naas town centre
locations.rationale: A solid control town with real services and credible daily convenience; should beat rural traps and many corridor locations.

locations.name: Athlone centre
locations.rationale: Another control town: not meant to dominate the model, but should clearly outperform weak-service rural candidates.

locations.name: Ennis centre
locations.rationale: A believable real-service town centre that provides a sanity anchor between suburbs and remote settlements.

locations.name: Bangor centre
locations.rationale: Coastal control town with substantial real services; should score strongly without eclipsing the best quiet suburbs.
